options.why: A common multiple of $6$ and $8$, but not the lowest — $24$ is smaller.
Found the highest common factor of $6$ and $8$ instead of the lowest common multiple.
Added $6 + 8$; the LCM is a shared multiple, not the sum.
Chose the smaller number; that is the HCF, since $6$ divides $12$.
Doubled $12$; $24$ is a common multiple but not the lowest.
Multiplied $6 \times 12$; when one number divides the other the LCM is just the larger number.
Found the highest common factor $1$ instead of the lowest common multiple.
Added $4 + 5$; the LCM is a shared multiple, not the sum.
Doubled the product; $20$ is already the lowest common multiple of these coprime numbers.
Multiplied $12 \times 18$ without dividing by the HCF $6$.
Gave the HCF of $12$ and $18$ rather than their LCM.
Divided the product by $3$ instead of the full HCF $6$.
Multiplied $18 \times 24$ without dividing by the HCF $6$.
Divided the product by $3$ instead of the full HCF $6$.
Gave the HCF of $18$ and $24$ instead of their LCM.
